creator: iwtcex@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Rainer Hurling from comment #3)

> Is there any chance of adapting the steam-utils for 64bit in the longer term or will it have to stay at 32bit?

This is simply upstream policy: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/steam-for-linux/issues/3518. Valve doesn't really _need_ to keep the launcher 32-bit, but it kind of works as a sort of insurance policy against people sabotaging Steam's ability to run old games by installing it in a pure 64-bit environment, so Valve isn't in a hurry to switch either. They seem to be slowly moving towards eliminating this requirement with https://gitlab.steamos.cloud/steamrt/steam-runtime-tools/-/blob/main/docs/container-runtime.md, but they are probably not quite there yet.
